label: Inventory Service
description: Checks real-time stock availability for line items before an order is accepted.

calls.label: Check stock availability for all line items
calls.purpose: Verify that every line item in the order is in stock at the requested quantity before committing to the order.

reliability.fallback: Return 409 Conflict — treat inventory service unavailability as stock unavailability

label: Payment Service
description: Creates Stripe PaymentIntents to initiate the payment flow. The Order Service creates a PaymentIntent before persisting the order; the customer completes payment on the client side.

calls.label: Create a Stripe PaymentIntent for the order total
calls.purpose: Obtain a Stripe client_secret that the browser uses to complete payment. Must succeed before the order is persisted.

reliability.fallback: Return 503 Service Unavailable — payment system temporarily unavailable

label: Orders PostgreSQL
description: Primary data store for order records. Owns the orders table and the outbox table used for transactional event publishing.

calls.label: Insert new order record
calls.purpose: Persist the order with AWAITING_PAYMENT status alongside the outbox entry for order.created.

calls.label: Fetch order by orderId
calls.purpose: Load order for status checks and event processing.

calls.label: Update order status
calls.purpose: Transition order to CONFIRMED, PAYMENT_FAILED, or other states on event receipt.

label: Event Bus (Kafka)
description: Platform Kafka cluster. Used to publish order lifecycle events (order.created, order.confirmed, order.cancelled) via the transactional outbox pattern.

calls.label: Publish order lifecycle events
calls.purpose: Notify downstream services (fulfilment, notifications, inventory reservation) of order state changes.

reliability.fallback: Outbox pattern — events written to outbox table atomically with the order record; a background worker publishes them. Kafka unavailability does not fail the order creation.

label: Session Cache (Redis)
description: Redis used for per-user rate limiting via a sliding window counter. Tracks the number of orders placed by each user in the last 24 hours.

calls.label: Increment order count for user
calls.purpose: Check and increment the per-user order count. Rejects if count exceeds 5 per 24 hours.

calls.label: Decrement order count on payment failure
calls.purpose: Restore the user's quota when a payment fails — a failed payment should not count against their daily limit.

reliability.fallback: Fail-open — allow the order to proceed if Redis is unavailable. Rate limiting is best-effort.
